It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} * {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11395853|ltq=Q11395853|qid=Q11395853|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 180 ** P155: {{ill|Fusuno Shrine|lt=Fusuno Shrine|qid=Q135041100|1=Fusuno Shrine}} ** P156: coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.304903|132.903409}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q272993|ltq=Q272993|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182950 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11545372|ltq=Q11545372|qid=Q11545372|1=Senior Sixth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひいじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3280005006144 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Hiinohayahikono Shrine|lt=Hiinohayahikono Shrine|qid=Q135099008|1=Dōsha-ni-imasu Hiiwaya-hiko Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県雲南市木次町里方字宮崎463', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県雲南市木次町里方463', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '同上', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:斐伊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11500716}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hii Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Unnan}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Unnan|Q877935|qid=Q877935|1=Unnan}} Kisuki-chō Satokata, Miyazaki 463 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inada-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Itsu no Ohabari-no-Mikoto
Hisahaya-hiko-no-Mikoto
Mikasuhino-Mikoto
Hokaguhibiko-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿 = Taisha-zukuri |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Shinkōsai
Mitane-sai (Koden Festival) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Hii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hii Shrine''' (ひいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Unnan|Q877935|qid=Q877935|1=Unnan}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is also called Miyazaki Daimyōjin. The ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}'' records two shrines called Hi-no-yashiro with officials from the Jingikan present, and these two are thought to have been merged into one by the time of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, becoming today’s Hii Shrine. Hii Shrine and the co-located Hiihayahiko Shrine in the Engishiki are identified with the present shrine. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|UNKNOWN|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Itsu no Ohabari|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsu no Ohabari-no-Mikoto}}. In the combined shrine hall (Hiihayahiko Shrine and Hisahaya-hiko Shrine), Hisahaya-hiko-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Mikahayahi-no-Kami|Q11574224|qid=Q11574224|lt=Mikasuhino-Mikoto|1=Mika-haya-hi}}, and {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|UNKNOWN|lt=Hokaguhibiko-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === The foundation year is unknown. According to old shrine traditions, it is extremely ancient, and {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|qid=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) is said to have been divided from Hii Shrine in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|qid=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. However, Hikawa Shrine’s side states that it was divided from Kizuki Taisha ({{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}}), located upstream on the Izumo Hika ({{ill|lt=Hii River|Q11500714|qid=Q11500714|1=Hii River}}). Although {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} is not geographically related to the present {{ill|lt=Hii River|Q11500714|qid=Q11500714|1=Hii River}}, in ancient times the Hii River flowed directly into the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|qid=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} instead of into {{ill|lt=Lake Shinji|Q1151248|qid=Q1151248|1=Lake Shinji}}, and Izumo Taisha lay downstream rather than upstream. Hii Shrine has always stood upstream on the Hii River. Under the Engi system it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and on {{ill|lt=November 10|Q2988|qid=Q2988|1=November 10}} of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13 under {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|qid=Q349243|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, it was granted divine rank of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|1=UNKNOWN}}. In {{ill|lt=May|Q119|qid=Q119|1=May}} of {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|qid=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}}, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Yahonsugi (Eight Cedars) === About 100 meters west of the main shrine is an outlying precinct called Yahonsugi. This place is traditionally said to be where Susanoo-no-Mikoto defeated the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also thought to be the site of one of the two shrines before they were merged. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Unnan, Shimane]]
